qas.q: Meeting regulations, recommendations and expectations of federal and state agencies that pay for health care services and regulate the industry
What is the first step health insurance specialists should take toward achieving compliance so they do not violate laws, which may result in penalties or fines
qas.a: identifying the laws that regulate the industry.

qas.q: What is the primary purpose of HIPAA Title I: Insurance Reform
qas.a: To provide continuous insurance coverage for workers and their insured dependents when they change or lose jobs

qas.q: The focus on the health care practice setting and reduction of administrative costs and burdens are the goals of which part of HIPPA
An independent organization that receives insurance claims form the physicians office, performs edits and transmits claims to insurance carriers is known as
Under HIPAA guidelines, a health care coverage carrier, such as BCBS that transmits health info in electronic form in connection with a transaction is called
Dr John Doe contracts with an outside billing company to manage claims and a/r. Under HIPAA guidelines, the biling company is considered a _____of the providers
An individual designated to assist the provider by putting compliance policies and procedures in place and training office staff is knows as a/an_______under HIPAA guidelines
If you give, release, or tranfer info to another entity, it is known as
Protected Health Information PHI is
qas.a: Any info that identifies an individual and describes his/her health status, age, sex, ethnicity, or other demographic characteristics, whether that info is or is not stored or transmitted electronically

qas.q: Unauthorized release of a patient's health information is called
A confidential communication related to the patient's treatment and progress that may be disclosed only with the patient's permission is known as
Under HIPAA exceptions to the right of privacy are those records involving
qas.a:
a. When the patient is a member of a managed care organization and the physician has signed a contract with the MCO
b.When patients have certain communicable diseases
c.When a medical device malfunctions
d.Criminal investigation/locating a missing person, material witness, suspect
e.When records are subpoenaed or there's a search warrants
f.Suspicious death or suspected crime victim-providers must report cases
g.Workers Comp
f.State Law (police), child or elder abuse, domestic violence, gunshot wounds

qas.q: At a patient's first visit, under HIPAA guidelines, the document that must be given so the patient acknowledges the provider's confidentiality of his or her PHI is the
If a breach of privacy is discovered, the healthcare provider is required to take affirmative action to respond to the breach and alleviate the severity of it. This is known as
Name the three main sections of the HIPAA Security Rule for protecting electronic health information
qas.a:
Administrative safeguards
Technical safeguards
Physical safeguards

qas.q: Name the three specific areas of significant change that resulted from the HITECH Health Information Technology for Economic and Clinical Health Act.
qas.a:
Business associates.
Notification of breach.
Civil penalties for non compliance with provisions

qas.q: One of the agencies charged with enforcing laws that regulate the health care industry is the
HIPAA transaction standards apply to the following, which are called covered entities:
qas.a:
heath care third party payers
health care providers
health care clearinghouses

qas.q: Enforcement of the privacy standards of HIPAA is the responsibility of the
qas.a: HCFAP Health Care Fraud and Abuse Control Program

qas.q: Verbal or written agreement that gives approval to some action, situation, or statement is called
An individuals formal written permission to use or disclose his or her personally identifiable health information for purposes other than treatment, payment, or health care operations is called
The NPP Notice of Privacy Practices document is given to patients
qas.a: at the first visit to the practice

qas.q: Privacy regulations allow patients the right to obtain a copy of PHI
qas.a: only if the health care provider has determined that it would be appropriate and would not endanger the patient or any other person

qas.q: Under HIPAA, patient sign in sheets
qas.a: are permissible but limit the information that is requested

qas.q: Under HITECH, if a breach occurs, the covered entity
qas.a: must notify the affected party no later than 60 calendar days after the discovery of the breach

qas.q: The HIPAA Omnibus Rule enhanced
Measurable solutions that have been taken, based on accepted standards, and are periodically monitored to demonstrate that an office is in compliance with HiPAA privacy rules are referred to
Stealing money that has been entrusted in ones care
Under the Criminal False Claims Act, fines and imprisonment penalties for making a false claim in connection with payment for health care benefits can be imposed on
qas.a: anyone who knowingly and willfully participated in the scheme

qas.q: The FCA provision that allows a private citizen to bring civil action for a violation on behalf of the federal government and share in any money recovered is referred to as
The initiative that established hotlines for the public to report issues that might indicate fraud, abuse or waste is

Health care providers who determine that they have submitted false claims should resolve the issue by seeking the Dept of Health and Human Services (HHS) and OIG guidance established in 2006 and referred to as
The OIG recommends that health care staff should attend trainings in "general " compliance
When faced with discovery of an offense or an error, health insurance specialists should immediately report concerns
qas.a: using the established chain of command outlined in their compliance plan
